paper_id: Amaral et al. - 2020 - A Reference Ontology of Money and Virtual Currencies
input_limitations: The markdown extraction contains figure placeholders and minor character encoding artifacts, but the main text is readable.

models.model_label: Reference Ontology of Money and Virtual Currencies

evidence_quotes.quote: This research aims at tackling these questions by conducting an ontological analysis of money and related concepts, grounded in the Unified Foundational Ontology, based on a literature review of the most relevant economic theories, and considering recent innovations in the financial industry.
In this paper, we extend this ontology to consider monetary credit-related concepts, including electronic monetary credit, and improve the considerations on both exchange and purchasing power.
In addition, we characterize the concept of virtual currencies and differentiate them from legal tender money.
In this section, we present a well-founded ontology that formalizes the characterization of money and currency, as well as its embedded concepts and relations.

goals.justification: The authors explicitly state that the model is created through ontological analysis, characterization, formalization, and differentiation of money, currency, and virtual currency concepts. This directly matches the domain-understanding goal.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology presented here can serve as a basis for future business ontologies and as a conceptual foundation for several types of information analysis and data integration.

goals.justification: The paper frames the ontology as a foundation for business ontologies, information analysis, and data integration. This is an intended use for integration and downstream artifact development, although no implemented system is reported.

evidence_quotes.quote: As a result, we propose a concrete artifact, namely, a Reference Conceptual Model (Reference Ontology) of Money and Virtual Currencies, which is specified in OntoUML [17] and thus, compliant with the ontological commitments of the Unified Foundational Ontology (UFO) [17].
The ontology presented here can serve as a basis for future business ontologies and as a conceptual foundation for several types of information analysis and data integration.

goals.justification: The model is explicitly presented as a reference ontology and a concrete artifact for future reuse. This supports a documenting/reference function, though the paper does not describe a separate documentation repository or knowledge base.
